raw_consumer_statement:
While shopping for XXXX XXXX online on XX/XX/XXXX, I believed I was on the company website and entered my credit card information to purchase shoes. However the transaction was 'declined ' as were several later attempts with other credit cards. I contacted the official XXXX company customer support and was informed I had been shopping on a fraudulent website. I immediately contacted Citibank MasterCard about the fraudulent website and was told to wait and see if any fraud charges occur.

No fraudulent charges appeared until I checked the account on XX/XX/XXXX and discovered ( without notice from Citibank ) XXXX fraud charges for {$19.00} each ( a few for less than {$10.00} ) that total {$9600.00} from XXXX XXXX in XXXX, XXXX posted on XX/XX/XXXX and XXXX. 

I immediately contacted Citibank Fraud Department and I spend an hour with the representative as she registered each transaction as disputed and also sent a new card. I explained the situation and said I did not recognize XXXX XXXX, never ordered anything from them and never received anything from them. 

After XXXX very long phone discussions in XXXX with numerous Citibank representatives we could not obtain further information on the investigation other than 'in process ' and it may take XXXX to XXXX billing cycles to complete. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I discovered the investigation had concluded with no fraud found and I was responsible for {$9600.00} ( after XXXX transaction for {$19.00} was closed in XXXX with a credit to my account ). I was told since I had given my card information to the merchant and therefore it was not fraud and I was responsible. I repeatedly told Citibank that I had NOT provided my card information to the merchant but I could get no further resolution nor explanation as to when this was decided and how could I appeal this erroneous decision.
